title: Today's Markets Brief: Is Wall Street’s AI boom spreading to main Street?
summary: A look at the spread of AI from Wall Street to main Street, and its impact on markets.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the latest market trends. Today, I'm checking out if Wall Street's AI boom is spreading to main Street. Let's dive in. I've been following this story for a bit, and it's fascinating to see how AI is making waves in the market.

First up, there's a story from Investing.com about Wall Street's AI boom potentially spreading to main Street. The idea here is that AI is no longer just a Wall Street phenomenon, but could be making its way into the broader market. I'm curious to see how this plays out, and what it might mean for investors.

Next, MarketWatch reports that Bitcoin is suffering from an 'attention deficit' as momentum traders have moved on. One analyst notes that 'the AI trade is sucking the blood out of crypto.' It's like the market is saying, 'AI is where it's at, and crypto is so last season.' This shift in attention is interesting, and it's not hard to see why AI is getting so much love.

Another story that's been making the rounds is the potential for AI to disrupt various industries. From what I've seen, it's clear that AI is a rapidly advancing field, and investors are eager to get in on the action. I'm excited to see where this trend takes us.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
